Parallel function
You’re building a Streamlit app that calls three AI models. Right now, they run one after the other. With one word — parallel=True — they all run at once.
That’s not an exaggeration. Streamlit 1.58 shipped one of the most quietly powerful features I’ve seen in any Python web framework: concurrent fragment execution.
What is @st.fragment?
A fragment is a decorated function that re-runs independently without triggering a full app rerun. Great for isolating expensive parts of your UI. But until now, multiple fragments still ran sequentially.
💡 New in 1.58
Set parallel=True on any fragment and it runs in its own thread, concurrently with every other parallel fragment on the page.
import streamlit as st
import time# Before: these run one after the other (6 seconds total)
# After: with parallel=True, all run at once (2 seconds total)
@st.fragment(run_every=5, parallel=True)
def sentiment_panel():
with st.spinner("Running sentiment model..."):
time.sleep(2) # simulate model call
st.success("Sentiment: Positive 😊")
@st.fragment(run_every=5, parallel=True)
def risk_panel():
with st.spinner("Running risk scorecard...")…
